objectives: Discover and map all target domains and assets
Identify potential data sources and information gathering tools
tools: Subdomain listing tools (e.g. Subdomain Finder, Domaintyper)
Reverse image search tools (e.g. Google Images, TinEye)
Social media and online presence mapping tools (e.g. Ahrefs, Moz)
success_criteria: All target domains are discovered and mapped
All identified data sources and tools are documented and integrated into the testing framework

objectives: Identify potential vulnerability classes and attack vectors
Analyze target domains and assets for known vulnerabilities and weaknesses
tools: Smart contract analysis tools (e.g. Etherscan, MythX)
Cross-chain bridge security tools (e.g. Chainalysis, Binance Smart Chain Scan)
success_criteria: All identified potential vulnerability classes and attack vectors are documented and prioritized
All target domains and assets are analyzed for known vulnerabilities and weaknesses

objectives: Test hypotheses with payloads and identify potential exploit vectors
Develop and refine exploit code and payloads
tools: Smart contract exploitation frameworks (e.g. Etherscan, MythX)
Cross-chain bridge security frameworks (e.g. Chainalysis, Binance Smart Chain Scan)
Exploit development and testing tools (e.g. Geth, Truffle)
success_criteria: All test hypotheses are validated and potential exploit vectors are identified
All developed and refined exploit code and payloads are documented and integrated into the testing framework

objectives: Validate and prioritize findings based on severity and impact
Analyze and classify findings according to the Immunefi Vulnerability Severity Classification System V2.3
tools: Vulnerability classification and prioritization tools (e.g. Burp Suite, ZAP)
Smart contract analysis and validation tools (e.g. Etherscan, MythX)
success_criteria: All findings are validated and prioritized based on severity and impact
All findings are classified according to the Immunefi Vulnerability Severity Classification System V2.3

objectives: Document and prepare submissions for the bug bounty program
Ensure all findings meet the program rules and guidelines
tools: Bug bounty reporting and submission tools (e.g. Bugcrowd, HackerOne)
Documentation and formatting tools (e.g. Markdown, Google Docs)
success_criteria: All findings are documented and prepared for submission
All submissions meet the program rules and guidelines

priority_targets: Proxy pattern implementations in Threshold Network
Access control vulnerabilities in Threshold Network
risk_considerations: Potential impact of critical smart contract vulnerabilities on user funds
Risk of oracle manipulation and front-running on tBTC
Risk of cross-chain bridge security breaches on Threshold Network
Risk of proxy pattern implementations being exploited for reentrancy attacks
Risk of access control vulnerabilities being exploited for unauthorized access to user funds
